programme.title: The Aviators S2 - "Cessna 195"
programme.desc: Kurtis takes us up in the aircraft that’s a crowd favorite wherever it he takes it. We spend some time with an ever-growing group of people who use their planes as campers and for one week a year, the tower at Oshkosh’s Wittman Regional Airport becomes the busiest on the planet.

programme.title: The Aviators S3 - "WWII Pilot Training - T-6"
programme.desc: Kurtis earn his wings in a T-6 warbird at Stallion 51 Flight Operations in Kissimmee, FL!

programme.title: Rethink S1 - "How does sword swallowing work?"
programme.desc: In a world where nothing is what it seems, there is a lot to discover. Have you ever had an eye floater, let’s examine what they are and how often they happen. How does sword swallowing work and how to use Jump Cuts in video editing.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Glima - Bjørn Braathen"
programme.desc: Bjørn Braathen is a young man and a passionate Glima (Norwegian wrestling) champion. This is the Art of the Norwegian Vikings, the special forces of their time.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Shaolin Kung Fu - Kirby Koo"
programme.desc: Kirby Koo is a Shaolin Kung Fu Martial Artist and yoga teacher who lives in New York City and trains with Shi Yan Ming, a 34rd generation Shaolin warrior monk.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Kapap - Amit Himelstein"
programme.desc: Amit Himelstein is the head instructor of the IKF and the leader in Kapap. His big motto? Respect everyone but fear no-one. He’s the embodiment of krav maga.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Kickboxing - Denise Kielholtz"
programme.desc: Denise Kielholtz is 6 time world champion kickboxer. Her goal? To become the world’s first female MMA world champion and will do anything to achieve this.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Future of Living Part 1"
programme.desc: Learn about future of living at the International Furniture Fair Cologne: vertical cities, new housing concepts & home innovations.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "The future of communication"
programme.desc: Check the future of communication and satellite connection to the Internet in all parts of the world, especially on cruise ships.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "High Tech Everyday"
programme.desc: With the technology we use daily, technology companies are trying to innovate and make their inventions more user-friendly in a digital and internet-connected world.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Future of Living Part 2"
programme.desc: Learn about trends and technologies in the smart kitchen, the digitization of living spaces and changes due to the pandemic and the home office.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S2 - "Paint Your Red River Stagecoach at High Noon"
programme.sub-title: Paint Your Red River Stagecoach at High Noon
programme.desc: Terry and Larry will test different famous shots from westerns. Can you shoot a cowboy hat off the bad guy? Will a shotgun blast really throw you back through a window? Can a single shot from a colt revolver break shackles?

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S2 - "Terry's Underworld"
programme.desc: Terry and Larry are going to test a famous scene from Underworld with Kate Beckensale. Terry will attempt to shoot a circle around himself in the floor and fall through to save himself from the pack of werewolves.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S3 - "A Pirates Life for Terry"
programme.desc: Terry and Larry go on a swashbuckling adventure when they test two scenes from the 1920’s film, The Black Pirate starring Douglas Fairbanks, Senior. Terry will see if he can knock down a ships mast with a canon, and then test gravity as he slides down a sail with only a knife.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S3 - "Terry Loves 70's Cop Shows"
programme.desc: Terry travels back in time to the 70’s where he gets to relive his childhood dreams of becoming a 70’s TV cop. Larry helps Terry test several scenarios, including hiding behind a car door for cover, and if you can really shoot a gas tank from a car to make it explode.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Surgery"
programme.desc: Hundreds of years before antibiotics, the legendary Chinese physician Hua Tuo performed miraculous surgical operations. Today, doctors perform increasingly complex surgery that is only possible with the support of advanced robotics.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Sports"
programme.desc: Sports and competitions have always been a core aspect of humanity’s development. While modern sports are evolving thanks to technology, the line between digital and reality has become blurry.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Food"
programme.desc: Large factory farms produce food for the masses with one caveat; they’re terrible for the environment. In an attempt to remedy this, chefs and scientists are working together to create a future where what we eat is as sustainable as it is delicious.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Comedy"
programme.desc: Comedy has always been a way for people to make sense of the world around them. As the world gets more complicated, how will comedy continue to evolve?

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Volcanic Wedding"
programme.desc: Host George Kourounis has braved many terrifying phenomena – but none as frightening as the one he takes on in this episode: marriage!

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Wild Water"
programme.desc: George travels to Guadaloupe Island off the coast of Mexico to dive with 16 foot Great White Sharks. He then uses drysuit and kayak to retrace the explorations of Tom Woodward into a flooded Northern Ontario cave in the middle of winter.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Avalanche"
programme.desc: Avalanche danger is very high in the Rogers Pass area of the Trans Canada Highway in British Columbia. Host of Angry Planet George Kourounis travels with a variety of experts to bring down huge avalanches in the Rocky Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Blizzard"
programme.desc: Our host George becomes a “human popsicle”, encased in ice in at the National Resarch Council’s high speed wind tunnel. Then he and his sidekick Mark Robinson climb to the top of the mountain in mid-January and brave their way into a summit blizzard.

programme.title: Extremes S1 - "Long Lives, Destructive Weather and Speed Records"
programme.sub-title: Long Lives, Destructive Weather and Speed Records
programme.desc: In this episode of Extremes, you will learn about extreme close-up photography, long lives, destructive weather and speed records.

programme.title: Extremes S1 - "Physics, Space and Wealth"
programme.desc: In this episode of Extremes, we’ll explore all things extreme in physics, space and wealth plus take a look at the smallest creatures on Earth.

programme.title: Extremes S1 - "Largest Animals, Human Bodies and Football"
programme.sub-title: Largest Animals, Human Bodies and Football
programme.desc: In this episode of Extremes, we will be exploring the largest animals, extreme facts about the human body and record breaking extremes in football and the field of music.

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science S1 - "Hot Hot Heat"
programme.desc: This week's experiments investigate how to send secret messages with heat, how a little bit of melted candle wax can turn into a bomb, and why heat is an essential ingredient in cooking an egg.

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science S1 - "Explosive Elements"
programme.desc: This week's experiments include recreating a big bang with lithium, the incendiary power of methane, and how a little bit of liquified nitrogen can lend a lot of power to a rocket.

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science S1 - "Colorful Conundrums"
programme.desc: This week's experiments highlight how electricity can tint titanium, how you can create a color wheel by lighting things on fire, and why the world gets all its colors from white light.

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science S1 - "Don't Believe Your Eyes"
programme.desc: This week's experiments delve into the possibilities of mind control, why objects and people tend to sync together, and the magical mysteries behind magnesium.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "LIKE BUTTON"
programme.desc: When first presented with the like button Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg had a simple response: “No.” He worried that it would discourage more meaningful online interaction. Eventually, his team changed his mind and transformed the world.

programme.title: Rock of Ages S1 - "Meteorites"
programme.desc: In this episode we go back in time to the edge of a 50.000-year crater. On a highway to Arizona, we followed the sign 'Meteor Crater'. This was the start for a fantastic tour to rocks from another world.

programme.title: Super Senses 2: Special Forces S1 - "THE SPECIALISTS"
programme.desc: Within this natural world lie countless hidden worlds, which each animal perceives in its own way, with its own highly tuned senses. These are the Specialists.

programme.title: Super Senses 2: Special Forces S1 - "AIR FORCES"
programme.desc: Some animals have the aerial advantage. They’re the fearless fliers that have the battlefield covered, scoping out the situation with super senses and the right stuff.

programme.title: The Rhino Orphanage S1 - "Pemba & Dwayne"
programme.desc: Too small to mix with others, Pemba needs a rhino friend his age, enter orphaned 34 day old, Dwayne. Under-staffed and over-worked, Karen is eager to get the two little guys living together, it should be easy - but little rhinos don’t always comply.

programme.title: Wild Ones S1 - "SMARTEST"
programme.desc: Wild Ones draws inspiration from the rhythms of nature, delving into the wonders of the animal kingdom. This captivating series explores the diverse behaviors that define survival—hunting and feeding strategies, family bonds, defense tactics, and the intricacies of courtship and mating. Who reigns supreme in the wild? Discover the untold stories of nature's fiercest competitors and most fascinating creatures.

programme.title: Wild Ones S1 - "WILD AUSTRALIA"
programme.desc: Wild Ones draws inspiration from the rhythms of nature, delving into the wonders of the animal kingdom. This captivating series explores the diverse behaviors that define survival—hunting and feeding strategies, family bonds, defense tactics, and the intricacies of courtship and mating. Who reigns supreme in the wild? Discover the untold stories of nature's fiercest competitors and most fascinating creatures.

programme.title: Wild Ones S1 - "MASTERS OF THE SKY"
programme.desc: Wild Ones draws inspiration from the rhythms of nature, delving into the wonders of the animal kingdom. This captivating series explores the diverse behaviors that define survival—hunting and feeding strategies, family bonds, defense tactics, and the intricacies of courtship and mating. Who reigns supreme in the wild? Discover the untold stories of nature's fiercest competitors and most fascinating creatures.

programme.title: Wild Ones S1 - "BIGGEST"
programme.desc: Wild Ones draws inspiration from the rhythms of nature, delving into the wonders of the animal kingdom. This captivating series explores the diverse behaviors that define survival—hunting and feeding strategies, family bonds, defense tactics, and the intricacies of courtship and mating. Who reigns supreme in the wild? Discover the untold stories of nature's fiercest competitors and most fascinating creatures.

programme.title: Blue World S4 - "Hawaiian Monk Seals, Wounded Warriors, and Counting Fish"
programme.sub-title: Hawaiian Monk Seals, Wounded Warriors, and Counting Fish
programme.desc: On a trip to Hawaii, Blue World meets the critically endangered Hawaiian Monk Seal, then travels to Bonaire to dive with a group of wounded veterans learning to dive. Finally, we participate in a fish census in Massachusetts.

programme.title: Blue World S4 - "Artificial Reefs and Free Diving with Karol Meyer"
programme.sub-title: Artificial Reefs and Free Diving with Karol Meyer
programme.desc: Blue World visits shipwrecks in Florida to see how they turn into artificial reefs. Then Blue World’s host learns to freedive with Karol Meyer…a world-record freediver.

programme.title: Blue World S4 - "Saving the Sturgeon and Carol Farming in Key Largo"
programme.sub-title: Saving the Sturgeon and Carol Farming in Key Largo
programme.desc: Blue World travels to upstate New York to learn how biologists are working to save the sturgeon in the St. Lawrence River. Then we visit an underwater farm in Key Largo, Florida, where they grow coral!

programme.title: Blue World S4 - "Belugas, Cousteau's Blue Hole, and Hawaiian Spinner Dolphins"
programme.sub-title: Belugas, Cousteau's Blue Hole, and Hawaiian Spinner Dolphins
programme.desc: Blue World learns how they train Beluga whales at the Mystic Aquarium, explores a deep blue hole in the Bahamas, and swims with wild Spinner dolphins in Hawaii.

programme.title: Monkey Thieves S3 - "Three’s a Crowd"
programme.desc: Childhood buddies Bipin, Yash and Tito continue to make inroads at Jaipur’s central fruit market, but a betrayal soon costs them their friendship.

programme.title: Quick Connections with Alie Ward S1 - "Hacking Happiness"
programme.desc: What could possibly link rockets and antidepressants? Are we on the cusp of having a neural switch that can control our moods? Alie Ward weaves a chain of extraordinary connections.

programme.title: Space Dealers S1 - "Dealers on a Mission"
programme.desc: Larry takes on an impossible mission: he’s been asked to find an item worn in space by Apollo 15 legend Al Worden. Torie searches for Russian space items for a museum in San Francisco and Cole goes on the hunt for an item from Apollo 13.

programme.title: Space Dealers S1 - "Money Trouble"
programme.desc: Larry gets the offer of a lifetime: a Soviet era space suit. Torie pays big for an Apollo 11 artefact but it’s not what it seems. Cole meets Apollo 7 legend Walt Cunningham.
